question_relevance_notes.botanics: Question targeted the specific plant species identified by the agent.
question_relevance_notes.regulatory: Question targeted specific infrastructure details visible in the image.
question_relevance_notes.meta: Question targeted the specific object (bin) and architectural features cited by the agent.

response_update_notes.botanics: Agent provided specific species identification (Phormium tenax) and confirmed its native status.
response_update_notes.regulatory: Agent provided specific details on curb profiles and driveway transitions.
response_update_notes.meta: Agent provided specific council examples (Auckland) and architectural details (whitewash).

verdict.judge_question_strategy_notes: The judge correctly identified the key differentiators (botanical and architectural) and targeted the relevant agents. However, the judge asked the Meta agent the same question twice in Round 1, wasting a turn. The questions were highly relevant to the agents' domains.
verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly synthesized the evidence to identify New Zealand. The reasoning was sound, though the coordinates provided (-36.84, 174.76) are in Auckland, whereas the ground truth is in the Bay of Plenty. The visual evidence (Phormium, architecture) supports the country identification well.
verdict.discussion_convergence_notes: The discussion successfully converged on New Zealand by confirming specific local markers (Phormium, bin design). The duplicate question to the Meta agent slightly reduced the efficiency of the convergence.
